records.conjecture: For all naturals n, m: (7n + 42m) mod 7 = 0.
For all naturals n, m: (4n + 4m) mod 2 = 0.
The sum of the first n positive multiples of 5 equals 5 times the n-th triangular number (stated as 2*sum = 5n(n+1)).
For all naturals n, n*(n+1) is even.
For all naturals n, n^3 + 2n is divisible by 3.
For all naturals n, m: (9n + 12m) mod 3 = 0.
For all naturals n, n^2 mod 2 has the same value as n mod 2 (a square is even iff its root is even).

Kolakoski self-description is sustainable: the two-pointer generator's read head stays strictly behind the write frontier with alphabet closed under {1,2}, forever (inv_gen); hence the generator is productive (unbounded growth). Witnesses: the generated prefix matches A000002 and reads off as its own run-length description on a complete-run window.

discards.statement: The prefix relation on lists (isPrefix l1 l2 := ∃ l3, l2 = l1 ++ l3) is a partial order: reflexive, transitive, and antisymmetric.
discards.reason: antisymmetry needs a length-zero-implies-nil chain through several supporting core lemmas whose exact names/availability are less certain here; more proof surface than the selected sketch for the same branching purpose.

discards.statement: Peirce's law ((p → q) → p) → p is a theorem of classical logic, provable via Classical.byContradiction. [T]
discards.reason: one-line term proof, but pulls in Classical.choice as an extra axiom beyond the propext/Quot.sound baseline already seen on the board, with no proof-effort saving over the selected sketch.

discards.statement: For all naturals n, gcd(n, n+1) = 1 (consecutive naturals are coprime).
discards.reason: requires unfolding Nat.gcd's well-founded recursion by hand since Mathlib's Coprime API is unavailable; lemma names needed for the induction are not confirmed to exist in core, higher availability risk than selected sketch.

this nonlinear-equality shape already produced two ledger failures this project (sumFives_formula, cube_plus_2n_div3) when closed via induction+omega without a ring-normalization tactic, which is unavailable without Mathlib; same risk class, avoided.

discards.statement: There is no injective function from Fin (n+1) to Fin n (pigeonhole principle).
discards.reason: needs a hand-rolled injectivity definition plus a counting/case argument over Fin with no Fintype/Finset support available; larger proof surface than the selected sketch.

discards.statement: Insertion sort on a list of naturals produces a result that is sorted and is a permutation of the input.
discards.reason: the permutation half needs either core List.Perm, whose availability without Mathlib is unconfirmed, or a hand-rolled multiset-count equality, adding a whole second lemma family beyond the selected sketch's single counting argument.

discards.statement: List.reverse is an involution: l.reverse.reverse = l.
discards.reason: duplicates an existing core-library lemma (List.reverse_reverse); no independent proof content beyond restating it.

discards.statement: Every tournament (complete asymmetric relation) on a finite vertex set has a Hamiltonian path. [T]
discards.reason: needs finite-graph/tournament machinery (Fintype, induction over vertex sets) not available without Mathlib; expected proof length far exceeds every other sketch considered.
